description: This is an abstract graphic illustrating the concept of a "splitted change" in land cover features within the context of land cover mapping. The image displays two irregular, roughly pentagonal polygons, each shaded in beige, representing distinct spatial areas or land cover features. These two polygons are positioned such that they partially overlap. The area where the two beige polygons intersect is highlighted in red. This red overlapping area signifies a new established feature resulting from a "splitted change," which occurs when a previously unmapped or small land cover feature (e.g., below the Minimum Mapping Unit (MMU) in 2012) combines with a changing portion of an adjacent larger feature (e.g., a part of an urban area changing into an industrial area by 2018) to form a new, mappable entity.
